hor_eq_hor'.solution:
Lemma hor_eq_hor' :
  hor = hor'.
Proof using.
  apply functional_extensionality. intros H1.
  apply functional_extensionality. intros H2.
  unfold hor, hor'. apply hprop_eq. intros h. split.
  { intros (b&Hb). destruct b; auto. }
  { intros [M|M]. { exists true. auto. } { exists false. auto. } }
Qed.
hor_eq_hor'.explanation: Show the two definitions of hor are equivalent by case analysis on the boolean witness.

hor_comm.solution:
Lemma hor_comm : forall H1 H2,
  hor H1 H2 = hor H2 H1.
Proof using.
  intros. unfold hor. apply hprop_eq. intros h. split;
  intros (b&Hb); exists (negb b); rewrite if_neg in *; auto.
Qed.
hor_comm.explanation: Commutativity of hor: negate the boolean witness to swap H1 and H2.

MList_using_hor.solution:
Lemma MList_using_hor : forall L p,
  MList L p =
  hor (\[L = nil] \* \[p = null])
      (\exists x L' q, \[L = x :: L'] \* \[p <> null]
        \* (p`.head ~~> x) \* (p`.tail ~~> q) \* MList L' q).
Proof using.
  intros. apply himpl_antisym.
  { xchange MList_if. xif; intros C.
    { xpull. intros ->. unfold hor. xsimpl. exists true. xsimpl. auto. }
    { xpull. intros x L' q ->. unfold hor. xsimpl. exists false. xsimpl. auto. } }
  { unfold hor. xpull. intros b. destruct b.
    { xpull. intros -> ->. xchange <- MList_nil. }
    { xpull. intros x L' q -> Hp. xchange <- MList_cons. } }
Qed.
MList_using_hor.explanation: Express MList as a disjunction (hor) of the nil case and the cons case, using the boolean to distinguish.

hand_eq_hand'.solution:
Lemma hand_eq_hand' :
  hand = hand'.
Proof using.
  apply functional_extensionality. intros H1.
  apply functional_extensionality. intros H2.
  unfold hand, hand'. apply hprop_eq. intros h. split.
  { intros M. split; [apply (M true) | apply (M false)]. }
  { intros (M1&M2). intros b. destruct b; auto. }
Qed.
hand_eq_hand'.explanation: Show the two definitions of hand are equivalent by relating universal quantification over bool to conjunction.

hand_comm.solution:
Lemma hand_comm : forall H1 H2,
  hand H1 H2 = hand H2 H1.
Proof using.
  intros. unfold hand. apply hprop_eq. intros h. split;
  intros M b; specialize (M (negb b)); rewrite if_neg in *; auto.
Qed.
hand_comm.explanation: Commutativity of hand: negate the boolean to swap H1 and H2 in the universal quantification.

hwand_hpure_l.solution:
Lemma hwand_hpure_l : forall P H,
  P ->
  (\[P] \-* H) = H.
Proof using.
  introv HP. applys himpl_antisym.
  { xchange (hwand_cancel \[P] H). xsimpl. auto. }
  { applys himpl_hwand_r. xsimpl. }
Qed.
hwand_hpure_l.explanation: When P holds, the wand [P] -* H simplifies to H. Forward: cancel the wand with [P]. Backward: introduce the wand.

himpl_hwand_hpure_lr.solution:
Lemma himpl_hwand_hpure_lr : forall (P1 P2:Prop),
  (P1 -> P2) ->
  \[P1] ==> (\[P1] \-* \[P2]).
Proof using.
  introv M. applys himpl_hwand_r. xsimpl. auto.
Qed.
himpl_hwand_hpure_lr.explanation: If P1 implies P2, then [P1] entails the wand [P1] -* [P2].

himpl_hwand_hstar_same_r.solution:
Lemma himpl_hwand_hstar_same_r : forall H1 H2,
  H1 ==> (H2 \-* (H2 \* H1)).
Proof using.
  intros. applys himpl_hwand_r. xsimpl.
Qed.
himpl_hwand_hstar_same_r.explanation: H1 entails the wand H2 -* (H2 * H1) because H1 * H2 trivially entails H2 * H1.

hwand_cancel_part.solution:
Lemma hwand_cancel_part : forall H1 H2 H3,
  H1 \* ((H1 \* H2) \-* H3) ==> (H2 \-* H3).
Proof using.
  intros. applys himpl_hwand_r. xchange (hwand_cancel (H1 \* H2) H3). xsimpl.
Qed.
hwand_cancel_part.explanation: Partial cancellation: H1 from the context partially satisfies the wand (H1*H2) -* H3, leaving H2 -* H3.

hwand_frame.solution:
Lemma hwand_frame : forall H1 H2 H3,
  (H1 \-* H2) \* H3 ==> H1 \-* (H2 \* H3).
Proof using.
  intros. applys himpl_hwand_r. xchange (hwand_cancel H1 H2). xsimpl.
Qed.
hwand_frame.explanation: Framing into a wand: (H1 -* H2) * H3 entails H1 -* (H2 * H3).

hwand_inv.solution:
Lemma hwand_inv : forall h1 h2 H1 H2,
  (H1 \-* H2) h2 ->
  H1 h1 ->
  Fmap.disjoint h1 h2 ->
  H2 (h1 \u h2).
Proof using.
  introv M1 M2 D. lets M: hwand_cancel H1 H2.
  applys M. exists h1 h2. splits*.
Qed.
hwand_inv.explanation: Concrete heap-level elimination of the wand: if H1 holds of h1 and (H1-*H2) holds of h2 with disjoint heaps, then H2 holds of their union.

himpl_qwand_hstar_same_r.solution:
Lemma himpl_qwand_hstar_same_r : forall H Q,
  H ==> Q \--* (Q \*+ H).
Proof using.
  intros. applys qwand_equiv. xsimpl.
Qed.
himpl_qwand_hstar_same_r.explanation: H entails the postcondition wand Q --* (Q *+ H) because H * Q v trivially entails Q v * H.

qwand_cancel_part.solution:
Lemma qwand_cancel_part : forall H Q1 Q2,
  H \* ((Q1 \*+ H) \--* Q2) ==> Q1 \--* Q2.
Proof using.
  intros. applys qwand_equiv. xchange (qwand_cancel (Q1 \*+ H) Q2). xsimpl.
Qed.
qwand_cancel_part.explanation: Partial cancellation for postcondition wands, analogous to hwand_cancel_part.
